description_long: This monument features a central figure of a robed man holding a staff, elevated on a rectangular pedestal. Flanking the main figure are two cherubic statues, each positioned on their own smaller plinths and holding scrolls or banners. The entire structure is made of weathered stone, with visible signs of aging and patina, giving it a historic and solemn appearance. The base is broad and rectangular, supporting the weight of the figures above, and is adorned with simple moldings. The monument is likely intended as a commemorative or religious structure, commonly found in parks, plazas, or near churches. The overall composition is symmetrical, with the cherubs adding a decorative and symbolic element to the central figure.
description: A weathered stone monument with a central robed figure holding a staff, flanked by two cherubic statues on a broad rectangular base. The structure is ornate and symmetrical, with visible aging and patina.
description_view_0: Front view showing the central robed figure holding a staff, flanked by two cherubs on either side, all atop a broad rectangular base.
description_view_1: Side view highlighting the depth of the monument, with the central figure and one cherub visible in profile, showing the layered plinths.
description_view_2: Rear view displaying the backs of the central figure and cherubs, with the plain rear face of the pedestal and less visible detail.
description_view_3: Opposite side view similar to view 1, showing the monument's depth and the arrangement of the figures from the other side.
attribute_ornamentation: Decorative elements include two cherubic statues holding scrolls or banners, detailed robes on the central figure, and simple moldings on the pedestal. The ornamentation is classical and religious in style.
attribute_base_design: The base is a broad, rectangular stone platform with simple moldings and a slightly raised plinth for each figure. The structure appears solid and weathered, with some discoloration and patina.
attribute_tip_design: The tip is formed by the head and staff of the central robed figure, with no pointed or pyramidal obelisk tip; instead, the staff and figure's head serve as the highest point.
attribute_surrounding_features: Two cherubic statues on smaller plinths flank the central figure, integrated into the monument's design. No additional surrounding features such as fences or vegetation are visible.
